changes_made: Renamed k8s_audit_logging_enabled to k8s_apiserver_audit_logging_enabled for clarity and consistency with existing patterns
review_notes: The control AC-4(26) is applicable to Kubernetes as it involves auditing and logging actions related to information flow, which can be mapped to Kubernetes audit logging and network policy logging. The function names mostly follow the naming conventions, but one function required renaming for consistency with existing patterns. The functions are technically accurate and map well to Kubernetes security controls.
naming_improvements: Ensure resource names are specific, such as using 'apiserver' instead of 'audit' for audit logging related to the API server
title: Information Flow Enforcement - Information flow enforcement | audit filtering actions when transferring informa
step1_reasoning: The control AC-4(26) focuses on auditing and logging actions related to information flow. In Kubernetes, this can be mapped to ensuring audit logging is enabled and configured to capture relevant metadata, such as API server interactions and admission controller decisions. Additionally, NetworkPolicies can be configured to log allowed and denied traffic, which aligns with auditing information flow.
